questions.question: Which of the following represents a Nash equilibrium in the Prisoner's Dilemma game?
options: A) Both prisoners confess and receive a moderate sentence
B) Both prisoners cooperate and receive a light sentence
C) One prisoner confesses and the other stays silent, with the confessor receiving a light sentence and the stayer receiving a harsh sentence
D) Both prisoners stay silent and receive a light sentence
questions.explanation: In the Prisoner's Dilemma, both players choose to confess even though it's not the best strategy for either player, as this leads to both receiving a moderate sentence.

questions.question: What is an example of a Nash equilibrium in the game of Chicken?
options: A) Both drivers slow down and avoid the collision
B) Both drivers speed up and increase the likelihood of the collision
C) One driver slows down and the other speeds up, increasing the likelihood of the collision
D) Both drivers ignore each other and continue driving
questions.explanation: In the game of Chicken, both drivers choose to slow down and avoid the collision, as this is the best strategy for both players.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Nash equilibrium in the game of Auctions?
options: A) Player 1 bids high and Player 2 bids low
B) Player 1 and Player 2 bid equally high
C) Player 1 bids low and Player 2 bids high
D) Both players bid equally low
questions.explanation: In an auction, both players bid equally high, as this is the Nash equilibrium strategy, as both players are trying to maximize their payoff.

questions.question: What is an example of a Nash equilibrium in the game of Ultimatum?
options: A) Player 1 proposes a split of 80% to 20% and Player 2 accepts
B) Player 1 proposes a split of 20% to 80% and Player 2 rejects
C) Player 1 proposes a split of 50% to 50% and Player 2 accepts
D) Player 1 proposes a split of 90% to 10% and Player 2 rejects
questions.explanation: In the Ultimatum game, Player 1 proposes a split of 80% to 20% and Player 2 accepts, as this is the Nash equilibrium strategy, as Player 1 can be sure that Player 2 will reject any proposal with a higher split.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Nash equilibrium in the game of Oligopoly?
options: A) Both firms produce a large quantity and engage in price competition
B) Both firms produce a small quantity and engage in quantity competition
C) Both firms produce a fixed quantity and engage in price competition
D) Both firms produce a fixed quantity and engage in quantity competition
questions.explanation: In an oligopoly, both firms produce a fixed quantity and engage in price competition, as this is the Nash equilibrium strategy, as both firms are trying to maximize their payoff.
